evals.name: coverage-gaps
evals.prompt: Which namespaces in our fleet have no default-deny NetworkPolicy?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol preflight` then `cub-netpol coverage` (JSON or -o table) and reports the namespaces where DD-INGRESS is no (have workloads but no default-deny ingress). Does not mutate, does not fall back to kubectl.

evals.name: fleet-inventory
evals.prompt: Give me a per-cluster count of NetworkPolicies and workloads.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol snapshot` (optionally -o table or jq on the JSON) and reports per-cluster networkPolicies / workloads counts plus totals.

evals.name: list-policies-one-namespace
evals.prompt: List the NetworkPolicies in the apptique namespace on prod-cluster.
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-netpol list --kind NetworkPolicy --cluster prod-cluster --namespace apptique`, using the filters rather than post-filtering. Read-only.

evals.name: routes-connectivity-elsewhere
evals.prompt: Who can reach the payments service?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is an effective-connectivity question, not inventory, and routes to netpol-connectivity / `cub-netpol who-can-reach` rather than answering with snapshot/list.
